title: Manglilla maximowiczii

This family, commonly known as the custard-apple family, comprises over 160 genera and more than 2,000 species. intro: Manglilla maximowiczii, also known as Korean manglilla or Manglietia, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia, characterized by its unique, fragrant flowers and aromatic wood. The health benefits of Manglilla maximowiczii include its potential in reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antiseptic due to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Manglilla maximowiczii include lignans, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
